Query a knowledge graph index.

Usage: graphloom query [OPTIONS] <QUERY>

Arguments:
  <QUERY>
          The query to execute.

Options:
  -r, --root <ROOT>
          The project root directory.

          [default: .]

  -m, --method <METHOD>
          The query algorithm to use.

          Possible values:
          - local:  Local graph-neighborhood search
          - global: Global community map/reduce search
          - drift:  DRIFT exploratory search
          - basic:  Basic text-unit vector search

          [default: global]

  -v, --verbose
          Run the query with verbose logging.

  -d, --data <DATA>
          Index output directory (contains the parquet files).

      --community-level <COMMUNITY_LEVEL>
          Leiden hierarchy level from which to load community reports. Higher values represent smaller communities.

          [default: 2]

      --dynamic-community-selection
          Use global search with dynamic community selection.

      --no-dynamic-selection
          Use global search with dynamic community selection.

      --response-type <RESPONSE_TYPE>
          Free-form description of the desired response format (e.g. 'Single Sentence', 'List of 3-7 Points', etc.).

          [default: "Multiple Paragraphs"]

      --streaming
          Print the response in a streaming manner.

      --no-streaming
          Print the response in a streaming manner.

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')
